text: The look Vance gives happens at the time Trump says that line, and I'm not entirely sure why he did, but it could've been the way Trump structured his sentence. Trump was talking about the WWII veteran Buddy Taggart. He says, "Buddy earned many honors including a Purple Heart, and a bronze star, came home, started a family..." ...which isn't very clear sentence structure. In fact the line sounds more like, "and a bronze star came home, started a family"... like did the bronze star get a family together? Also, does "starting a family" really qualify to be included on a list of Buddy's "many honors"? It was just strange phrasing, and if I had to guess, maybe Vance and I shared the same moment of being perplexed.
